name: Fluke: PNW Orca Atlas
subtitle: Private on-device matching
description:
Explore sourced killer whale sightings, individual whale profiles, and historical context across the Pacific Northwest.

ON-DEVICE IDENTIFICATION
Point the live camera at an orca dorsal fin to compare an orca individual against a certified rights-cleared catalog. Frames are analyzed locally as they arrive; matching does not capture or upload them. Camera frames, photo crops, embeddings, the live candidate set, caches, and drafts stay on your device. Identification is enabled for this release only after the production catalog verifier reports ready:true.

SIGHTINGS
Browse attributed public observations. Explicit submission sends its contact email, selected location and content, attached photo, and selected suggestion evidence to Fluke for moderation. That evidence contains catalog ID, similarity score and score semantics, manifest/model/index versions, and up to five reference-photo IDs. Unselected candidates remain local. Queued drafts remain on device until Submit is tapped. Optional account use separately sends Name, account email, and User ID. Sign in with Apple sends an identity token and one-use authorization code for authentication.

WHALES AND ATLAS
Search the catalog, read evidence-backed profiles, and explore historical ranges and movement context. Confidence and source attribution stay visible.

Fluke has no advertising, analytics, or tracking. It is an independent reference project, not an emergency alert service. Never approach or pursue wildlife; follow current official viewing regulations.
promotionalText: Use private live-camera dorsal-fin matching for orca individuals, explore sourced PNW sightings, and browse evidence without analytics or tracking.
whatsNew: Version 1.1 adds gated live-camera dorsal-fin matching against a certified production catalog. Frames, crops, embeddings, and the live candidate set stay on device. Explicit sighting submissions send their contact email, chosen location and content, attached photo, and selected suggestion evidence (catalog ID, score and semantics, artifact versions, and up to five reference-photo IDs). Unselected candidates stay local.
reviewNotes:
Version 1.1 build 2 must not be submitted until the exact fluke-model mobile release report supplied to the package verifier reports ready:true. In Identify, point the live camera at an orca dorsal fin to review local suggestions. Camera frames are analyzed without capture or upload. Crops, embeddings, the live candidate set, caches, and drafts stay on device. A selected suggestion is not a confirmed identification. Only explicit sighting submission sends its contact email, user-chosen location and content, attached photo, and selected suggestion evidence: catalog ID, similarity score and score semantics, manifest/model/index versions, and up to five reference-photo IDs. Unselected candidates remain local. Fluke has no analytics or tracking. Optional account use separately sends Name, account email, and User ID. Sign in with Apple sends its identity token and one-use authorization code for authentication.

Suggested review path:
1. Browse Sightings and Whales.
2. Open Identify and point the live camera at a permitted test target to review local candidates from the certified catalog.
3. Select or dismiss a suggestion and observe that its on-device catalog provenance remains in the draft.
4. Draft a sighting and verify no network submission occurs until Submit is tapped.
5. Submit only test content suitable for moderation.

Live API: https://fluke-api.onrender.com
Support: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/support
Privacy: https://fluke-pnw.vercel.app/privacy
